scope: Pass B review of the Mention API reference. This bundle covers the legacy read streams plus app metadata and alert-scoped shares, preferences, and tasks that fit the declarative JSON read dialect. Remaining endpoints are detail duplicates, ID/filter-scoped subresources, statistics with repeated query-array filters, streaming endpoints, or account/alert/task/share/tag mutations.

excluded.reason: Superseded by /accounts/me, which is covered by the account_me stream.
Detail endpoints return the same alert, mention, share, or task object families as covered list streams and require caller-supplied IDs.
Mention children require a caller-supplied mention ID and are not a standalone account/alert collection.
The stream-mentions endpoint is a live streaming/polling surface, not a finite paginated JSON sync stream.
Authors require additional time/source/sort filters in the query string and are not emitted by the legacy connector's account/alert/mention/tag record contract.
Statistics require repeated query-array filters such as alerts[] and optional analytics selectors that the current stream.Query dialect cannot model without adding broad per-stream config.
Create endpoints mutate accounts, alerts, shares, tags, or tasks and need a dedicated reverse-ETL approval design; legacy Write is unsupported.
Update endpoints mutate account, alert, share, preference, tag, or task state and are intentionally not exposed by this read-only connector.
Mention curation changes read/favorite/relevance state and is not a safe source-read operation.
Pause/unpause and mark-all-read endpoints mutate alert processing or mention read state.
Delete endpoints remove accounts, alerts, shares, tags, or tasks and are intentionally not exposed.
